title: Multimodal neurophenomenology of advanced concentration absorption meditation: An intensively sampled case study of Jhana.

assets.abstract: Using a combination of fMRI, EEG, and phenomenology ratings, we examined the neurophenomenology of advanced concentrative absorption meditation, namely jhanas (ACAM-J), in a practitioner with over 23,000 h of meditation practice. Our study shows that ACAM-J states induce reliable changes in conscious experience and that these experiences are related to neural activity.
